data.name: DataEntry_Title
data.value: Entry of required information + Processed Metrics

data.name: DataEntry_Instructions
data.value: For proper execution of the analysis, you are required to complete only the cells shaded in gray and in bold.

data.name: RegisteredProperties_Tooltip
data.value: Total number of properties currently listed in the official property tax register/cadastre.

data.name: NonRegisteredProperties_Tooltip
data.value: Estimated properties not in the tax register. If unknown, check 'I don't have a specific number' to enter a percentage of registered properties.

data.name: EstimateCheckbox
data.value: I don't have a specific number (use estimate)

data.name: EstimatedProperties_Tooltip
data.value: Total estimated taxable properties in the jurisdiction = Registered + Non-registered.

data.name: CompliantProperties_Tooltip
data.value: Estimated using proxy formula to account for partial payments across fiscal year. Assumes proportion of compliant properties equals proportion of revenue collected.

data.name: TotalFiscalBase_Tooltip
data.value: Total value of all registered and taxable properties based on their fiscal (assessed) valuation. This is the officially recorded fiscal base used for property tax calculations.

data.name: TotalMarketValue_Tooltip
data.value: Estimated total market value of all taxable properties at real market prices. If unknown, check 'I don't have market value data' to enter estimated percentage above fiscal values.

data.name: MarketValueEstimateCheckbox
data.value: I don't have market value data (use estimate)

data.name: MarketValueHigherBy
data.value: Market value is higher than Fiscal Base by:

data.name: MarketValueEstimateHelper
data.value: e.g., 30% means Market Value = Fiscal Base × 1.30

data.name: BilledAmount_Tooltip
data.value: Amount officially charged for collection (billed) for the year under analysis.

data.name: OutstandingAmount_Tooltip
data.value: Portion of current year charges that remain unpaid. Should NOT include arrears from previous years. If exact data unavailable, provide reasonable estimate.

data.name: RevenueToDate_Tooltip
data.value: Auto-calculated. Total revenue collected = Billed Amount minus Outstanding. Updates dynamically.

data.name: AvgValues_Title
data.value: Useful Average Values required for the Analysis

data.name: AvgTaxableValue_Tooltip
data.value: Average official valuation per property, determined by cadastre. Base for applying tax rates.

data.name: AvgMarketValue_Tooltip
data.value: Estimate of average property value if assessed at market levels. Reflects theoretical tax base under ideal valuation.

data.name: EffectiveTaxRate_Tooltip
data.value: Actual effective rate from real revenue collected. Reflects under-assessment, exemptions, and compliance limitations.

data.name: AvgBilledAmount_Tooltip
data.value: Estimated billing per property based on current assessed values and effective tax rate.

data.name: PotentialBilling_Tooltip
data.value: Theoretical maximum billing per property assuming full market value assessment.

data.name: ComplianceRatio_Tooltip
data.value: Proxy indicator of compliance system performance. Measures proportion of billed amounts actually collected. >80% Green, 60-80% Yellow, <60% Red.

data.name: CoverageRatio_Tooltip
data.value: Proxy indicator of coverage system performance. Measures proportion of total properties registered. >80% Green, 60-80% Yellow, <60% Red.

data.name: ValuationRatio_Tooltip
data.value: Proxy indicator of valuation system performance. Measures how closely fiscal assessments match market values. >80% Green, 60-80% Yellow, <60% Red.

data.name: ValuationRatio_Formula
data.value: Total Fiscal Base / Total Market Value

data.name: PotentialRevenue_Title
data.value: Potential Revenue Breakdown (Actual Revenue + Adm. Gap breakdown)

data.name: ComplianceGap_Tooltip
data.value: The proportion of registered properties that do not pay the tax or fulfill their liability. It reflects weaknesses in enforcement, billing, and payment collection systems. The compliance gap equals Outstanding.

data.name: CoverageGap_Tooltip
data.value: The proportion of total estimated properties that are not registered in the property tax system. This gap indicates lack of property identification and inclusion in the fiscal database. The gap is quantified by multiplying the number of unregistered properties by the average billed amount per registered property.

data.name: ValuationGap_Tooltip
data.value: The valuation gap estimates how much more revenue could be billed to compliant properties if they were assessed at market value instead of undervalued fiscal assessments. It reflects weaknesses in the valuation framework and outdated property records.

data.name: MixedGapReg_Tooltip
data.value: This mixed gap captures the revenue loss from non-compliant properties that are registered but do not pay - and whose values are also below market. It shows the compounded effect of enforcement failure and undervaluation.

data.name: MixedGapUnreg_Tooltip
data.value: This mixed gap estimates the revenue missed from unregistered properties valued at market levels. It captures the full revenue loss due to both lack of registry and undervaluation.

data.name: MatrixDashboard_Description
data.value: This matrix visualization shows the property tax gap analysis across two dimensions: the number of properties (vertical axis from Compliant to Estimated) and the billing level (horizontal axis from average billed to achievable market-based billing). Each colored cell represents a different type of gap or revenue component. The same breakdown is also shown in the pie chart below.

data.name: RevenueAndAdmGaps_Description
data.value: This pie chart illustrates the same information as the matrix above in percentage form. It shows the share of total potential revenue corresponding to the revenue actually collected, as well as the contribution of each type of administrative gap (uncollected or lost revenue) to that total.

data.name: AdmGapsPie_Description
data.value: This pie chart illustrates the percentage distribution of the revenue gaps. It visually shows the contribution of each type of gap to the total uncollected revenue, helping to identify which gaps are the most significant.
